title: Better Semiconductor Stock for 2026: AMD vs. Intel
text: Motley Fool analysis comparing AMD and Intel for 2026 investment positioning highlights AMD's superior growth prospects. AMD's data center momentum, driven by EPYC server CPUs and Instinct AI GPUs, is expected to drive 34% revenue growth in 2026 and 43% in 2027. Intel, by contrast, faces slower near-term growth despite strategic initiatives to compete in data center and AI. AMD's market share gains in server CPUs (from 27% to 36% year-over-year) and successful MI300 GPU launch provide multiple growth vectors. The Motley Fool analysis suggests AMD offers better risk-adjusted returns for 2026, given its demonstrated execution in AI infrastructure and stronger visibility into customer demand. Intel faces execution risks with new process technology and competitive pressure from both AMD and NVIDIA. AMD's valuation, while elevated, is justified by its growth rate and market share expansion in strategic segments.
